tasks.description: Add partTypeByID tracking to DeltaState interface
steps: DeltaState interface at lines 33-38 includes partTypeByID: Map<string, 'text' | 'reasoning'>
createDeltaState() at line 693 initializes partTypeByID as new Map()

tasks.description: Create extractDeltaContent() function for flat delta event structure
steps: New function extractDeltaContent(event) exists near extractTextContent()
Function extracts {partID, delta, field} from flat content structure (NOT nested in properties.part)
Delta events are flat: content.partID, content.delta, content.field - NO type field
Function returns undefined for non-delta events
Function handles content.delta, content.partID, content.field

tasks.description: Track part types from message.part.updated events
steps: Only when event_type === 'message.part.updated' AND extractTextContent() returns result
Store part.type ('text' or 'reasoning') in state.partTypeByID[partID]
Map key is the partID, value is 'text' or 'reasoning'
This runs BEFORE delta processing so type map is populated

tasks.description: Handle message.part.delta events in aggregation loop
steps: Aggregation loop (lines 596-622) checks for message.part.delta event type
Delta events call extractDeltaContent() to get partID and delta text
Delta text is accumulated in state.textByPart by partID
Event is added to state.processedEventIds to prevent duplicates

tasks.description: Route deltas to thinking or textContent based on tracked part type
steps: Delta partID is looked up in state.partTypeByID to get type
If type is 'reasoning', partID is added to reasoningPartIDs
If type is 'text', partID is added to textPartIDs

tasks.description: Handle deltas for partIDs not yet in type map (race condition)
steps: If partID lookup in state.partTypeByID returns undefined
Default to treating as 'text' (add partID to textPartIDs)
Log warning for debugging: 'Delta for unknown part type, defaulting to text'

tasks.description: Merge tool input when non-empty input arrives
steps: Tool merge logic (lines 629-639) includes input merge
Condition: if (toolCall.input && Object.keys(toolCall.input).length > 0)
When condition met: existing.input = toolCall.input (replace, not deep merge)
Note: toolCall is NEW event state, existing is ACCUMULATED state from previous events
Empty input {} does not overwrite existing populated input
